title: HQ-SVC Enhancement Integration
description: Integrate HQ-SVC post-processing enhancement for Quality Pipeline. Provides decoupled codec architecture, super-resolution (22→44.1kHz), and DSP refinement for artifact reduction. Target: Speaker similarity ≥0.94, MCD ≤3.6.
design:
## Architecture
Seed-VC DiT → HQ-SVC Enhancement → Output
              ├─→ Decoupled Codec
              ├─→ Diffusion Refinement
              └─→ DSP Post-Processing (44.1kHz)

## Implementation
1. Create HQSVCEnhancer wrapper (src/auto_voice/inference/hq_svc_wrapper.py)
2. Integrate into SeedVCPipeline with enable_hq_enhancement flag
3. Add Web UI toggle
4. Benchmark quality and performance
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Speaker similarity ≥ 0.94 (improvement from ~0.87)
- [ ] MCD ≤ 3.6 (improvement from ~4.2)
- [ ] Super-resolution PESQ ≥ 4.0
- [ ] Web UI has "Enable HQ Enhancement" checkbox
- [ ] Pipeline routes: Seed-VC → HQ-SVC → output
- [ ] E2E tests pass with HQ enhancement
close_reason: Converted to conductor tracks - nsf-harmonic-modeling_20260201, hq-svc-enhancement_20260201

title: Formalize nested model repo ownership and hygiene
description:
Audit findings:
- `models/hq-svc`, `models/meanvc`, and `models/seed-vc` are tracked as gitlinks in the parent repo but there is no `.gitmodules` mapping.
- `models/hq-svc` contains live code edits in `utils/ddsp/vocoder.py` and `utils/models/content_encoder.py`.
- `models/meanvc` has mode-only churn across multiple files plus untracked checkpoint directories.
- `models/seed-vc` has untracked runtime artifacts (`.claude-flow/`, `__pycache__`, `=3.20.2`) inside the nested repo.

Needed work:
- decide whether these vendor repos should remain gitlinks, become proper submodules, or move to a documented vendor/import workflow
- document ownership and allowed local modifications for vendor repos
- clean ignored/runtime artifacts and normalize tracked file modes where appropriate
- make repo status/reporting unambiguous so local vendor dirt does not look like unfinished parent-repo product work
notes: Discovered while auditing remaining dirty work after closing AV-dkd and AV-0wz on branch ralph-session/fd7dbc33.

title: NSF Harmonic Modeling Integration
description: Integrate Neural Source Filter (NSF) for explicit harmonic/noise separation in singing voice conversion. Provides mcep decoupling, better pitch accuracy, and natural singing characteristics. Target: Pitch RMSE ≤15 cents, harmonic coherence ≥0.90.
design:
## Architecture
Content Encoder → DiT Decoder → NSF Module → Vocoder
                                 ├─→ Harmonic Generator (F0-driven)
                                 ├─→ Noise Generator (filtered)
                                 └─→ Source Filter (mcep envelope)

## Implementation
1. Implement NSF harmonic/noise generators (src/auto_voice/models/nsf_module.py)
2. Integrate into Quality Pipeline after DiT decoder
3. Add mcep and F0 losses to training
4. Benchmark pitch accuracy and naturalness
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pitch RMSE ≤ 15 cents (improvement from ~22 cents)
- [ ] Harmonic coherence ≥ 0.90
- [ ] Naturalness MOS ≥ 4.0
- [ ] Vibrato preservation: F0 variance ±10%
- [ ] NSF module toggleable via config
- [ ] E2E tests pass with NSF enabled

title: ECAPA2 Speaker Encoder Upgrade
description: Upgrade speaker style encoder from CAMPPlus to ECAPA2 for better zero-shot voice conversion, multilingual support, and robustness to background music. Target: Speaker similarity ≥0.92, EER ≤2.0%, SNR robustness ≥5dB.
design:
## Architecture
Reference Audio → Mel → Speaker Encoder → 192-dim Embedding
                         [User selectable]
                         ├─→ CAMPPlus (default)
                         └─→ ECAPA2 (robust)

## Implementation
1. Download ECAPA2 weights from FreeSVC/SpeechBrain
2. Create ECAPA2Encoder wrapper (src/auto_voice/models/ecapa2_encoder.py)
3. Project output to 192-dim for compatibility
4. Re-encode existing voice profiles with dual embeddings
5. Benchmark speaker similarity and robustness
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Speaker similarity ≥ 0.92 (improvement from ~0.87)
- [ ] EER ≤ 2.0% for speaker verification
- [ ] Robust to SNR ≥ 5dB (background music)
- [ ] Multilingual support (English, Spanish, Mandarin, Korean)
- [ ] Config flag: speaker_encoder: 'campplus' | 'ecapa2'
- [ ] E2E tests pass with ECAPA2
close_reason: Converted to conductor tracks - ecapa2-speaker-encoder_20260201, pupu-vocoder-upgrade_20260201

title: Pupu-Vocoder Anti-Aliasing Upgrade
description: Upgrade Quality Pipeline vocoder with Pupu-Vocoder anti-aliasing or integrate ADAA techniques into BigVGAN. Eliminates spectral artifacts and aliasing for cleaner high-frequency synthesis. Target: PESQ ≥4.3, aliasing reduction ≥50%.
design:
## Architecture
Seed-VC DiT → 128-band mel → Vocoder → Audio
                              [User selectable]
                              ├─→ BigVGAN v2 (default)
                              └─→ Pupu-Vocoder (anti-aliased)

## Implementation
1. Clone Pupu-Vocoder, download 44.1kHz weights
2. Create PupuVocoder wrapper (src/auto_voice/models/pupu_vocoder.py)
3. Add vocoder_type config parameter
4. Benchmark PESQ and spectral analysis
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PESQ ≥ 4.3 at 44.1kHz (vs BigVGAN ~4.1)
- [ ] High-frequency aliasing reduced ≥50%
- [ ] Spectral flatness improved
- [ ] Config flag: vocoder_type: 'bigvgan' | 'pupu'
- [ ] Performance ≤1.2x slower than BigVGAN
- [ ] E2E tests pass with Pupu vocoder
close_reason: Converted to conductor tracks - ecapa2-speaker-encoder_20260201, pupu-vocoder-upgrade_20260201

title: Resolve orphaned SSIM training loss module
description:
Audit findings:
- `src/auto_voice/training/ssim_loss.py` is untracked and currently unused.
- No production code imports `SSIMLoss`; the active SSIM path is the separate `_ssim_loss` implementation in `src/auto_voice/models/so_vits_svc.py`.
- This creates duplication risk and ambiguity about which SSIM implementation is intended for training.

Needed work:
- decide whether `src/auto_voice/training/ssim_loss.py` should be integrated into a real training path or removed
- if kept, add tests and wire one canonical caller
- if removed, confirm the existing So-VITS SSIM path is the only supported implementation and document that choice
notes: Discovered during post-completion audit after AV-dkd/AV-0wz. Current repo search shows no imports of `SSIMLoss`.
